facts_disclosed: Hong Kong trip with Nai Nai May 20 2026

turns.content: ok i have big news and i'm lowkey tearing up about it already
so you know how my nai nai came to australia from guangzhou back in the 70s right. she hasn't been back to that part of the world since. like not once. fifty something years.
That's a really long time to be away from where you grew up. What's happening?
i'm taking her to hong kong. we booked the flights today. leaving may 20th. i genuinely cannot stop crying about it lol this is so embarrassing but it just feels so big

That's not embarrassing at all — that's genuinely a huge and incredibly meaningful thing to do. Taking your grandmother back to that part of the world after fifty-plus years is extraordinary. What made you decide to do it now?

honestly she's getting older and i just kept thinking... if not now then when. like i can't take her back to guangzhou because that's a whole other level of logistics but hong kong is close enough and it's still cantonese speaking and she has some distant relatives there she hasn't seen in decades. my parents are helping with the cost but i'm paying for some of it from my cafe savings

The reasoning is so right — "if not now, when" is exactly the mindset for something like this. And Hong Kong being Cantonese-speaking is really important. She'll actually be able to communicate naturally everywhere, which would be so different from her daily life. How did she react when you told her?

ok so this is the part that wrecked me. she went really quiet at first and i thought maybe she didn't want to go or was scared or something. and then she just started crying. like proper crying. my nai nai does NOT cry. she's the toughest person i know. and she held my hand and said something in cantonese that my mum translated as basically 'i never thought i'd go back'

That is incredibly moving. For someone who's been that strong and stoic to have that kind of reaction — that tells you everything about how much this means to her. You're giving her something really profound.

i know and it's making me emotional just thinking about it again lol. like she left guangzhou when she was young and built this whole life in australia and raised kids and now grandkids and she just... never went back. there's so much there that i don't even fully understand about what she left behind

There's probably a whole chapter of her life story that she's never been able to fully share, especially across the language barrier. This trip might open that up in ways you don't expect. Do you have a rough plan for what you'll do there?

sort of. we're going for about ten days. i want to take her to some of the old neighborhoods, eat all the food obviously, visit the relatives. but also i kind of want to just... let her lead. like let her take me to the places she remembers or wants to see. it's her trip really. i'm just the logistics person lol

That's the perfect approach honestly. Letting her guide the experience means you'll see Hong Kong through her eyes, which will be completely different from any tourist experience. And you'll probably learn so much about her and your family history in the process.

that's exactly what i'm hoping for. also my cantonese better improve heaps between now and may because i'm going to need to help her navigate airports and stuff and i cannot be accidentally telling customs officers i'm pregnant lol. but genuinely this is the most meaningful thing i've ever done and i'm so glad we're doing it

Ha, maybe leave the pregnancy announcements out of the customs interaction. But seriously, you have a few months to practice and you'll probably pick up so much just from being immersed. This is a beautiful thing you're doing. May 20th is going to be a really special day.

thanks... genuinely. ok i need to go blow my nose because i've been crying for like twenty minutes lol. still need to figure out hotels and stuff but the flights are locked in and that's the important part. i'll probably be back to stress about logistics later
Take your time with the planning — you've got a good window before May. Go take care of yourself and enjoy the excitement. This is going to be an incredible trip.
